Skinnovate

Platform Overview

Skin intelligence.
Doctor precision.

A full-stack dermatology platform where patients upload skin images, an AI model returns an instant diagnosis, and certified dermatologists validate, prescribe, and manage care — all in one connected workflow.

React 18 Flask 3 TensorFlow / Keras PostgreSQL 16 JWT Auth Docker

End-to-end flow

Public
Register & Login
Patient
Upload skin photo
AI Model
Predict condition + confidence
Doctor
Review & validate diagnosis
Doctor
Add notes, prescriptions, treatment
Patient
Track records, treatments & appointments
Admin
Monitor & manage platform

Public

Pre-login

01

Landing Page

The public entry point of the platform. Communicates the value proposition at a glance and routes visitors into the registration or login flow.

Hero headline and sub-copy clearly state what the platform does — upload a skin image, get an AI diagnosis, connect with a dermatologist
Two primary CTAs: Start Free Analysis takes unregistered visitors to sign-up; Sign in routes returning users to login
Navigation bar exposes Login and Get Started for quick access from any scroll position
Landing

02

Registration & Login

New users create an account by entering personal details and selecting their role. On login, the backend reads the JWT claim and routes each user to their role-specific dashboard automatically.

Role selection at registration time determines which dashboard, routes, and data the user can access for the entire session
Passwords are hashed with bcrypt; access and refresh tokens are issued as a pair on every successful login
An Axios interceptor in the frontend transparently refreshes the access token on 401, keeping the session alive without forcing a re-login
Connects to → Patients land on the Patient Dashboard; doctors on the Doctor Dashboard; admins on the Admin Dashboard — all from the same login endpoint
Post-login routing

Patient

Role

03

Patient Dashboard

The patient's home screen. A personalised health summary that brings every dimension of their activity into one glanceable view — analyses, appointments, treatments, and loyalty points.

Four stat cards: total AI analyses submitted, scheduled appointments, active treatment plans, and accumulated loyalty points (earned at +5 per analysis)
Recent Analyses panel lists the latest AI results with condition, confidence score, severity badge, and live doctor review status — Awaiting Doctor Review or Doctor Validated
Upcoming Appointments panel shows the next scheduled visits with date, time, type, and status badge; emergency-flagged appointments are visually distinguished
Each panel links directly into its full dedicated page, making the dashboard a true navigation hub
Connects to → Analysis status shown here updates the moment a doctor validates or modifies the diagnosis on the Review Diagnosis page
Patient Dashboard

04

AI Skin Analysis

The core feature. The patient uploads a photo of the affected area and receives an instant AI-powered preliminary diagnosis — no waiting, no appointment needed for a first assessment.

Drag-and-drop upload zone accepts JPG, PNG, and WebP up to 16 MB
The image is preprocessed server-side through the exact pipeline used during model training: resize to 224×224, unsharp mask, CLAHE on the LAB L-channel, normalise
Returns predicted condition (Acne, Eczema, Milia, Rosacea), confidence score, severity rating, and a consultation flag when confidence falls below 70%
Full analysis history on the right: thumbnail of the uploaded image, condition, confidence, severity badge, and current doctor review status for every past submission
Once a doctor validates a result, its status in the history updates to Doctor validated in real time
Connects to → Every submitted analysis lands directly in the doctor's Patient Queue as a Pending Review card, awaiting validation
AI Analysis

05

Appointment Booking & Management

Full appointment lifecycle management. Patients book follow-up visits with available dermatologists and track every status transition from request through completion.

Book Appointment flow queries the backend for available doctor slots and lets the patient select date, time, and visit type (In Person or Video)
Emergency flag available during booking — triggers an Urgent badge visible to the assigned doctor in their schedule
Filter bar separates appointments into All, Scheduled, Requested, Completed, and Cancelled views
Cancel action directly on the appointment card for any visit that has not yet started
Connects to → Booked appointments appear in the doctor's Schedule and are surfaced in the Admin Dashboard's Pending Appointments panel
Appointments

06

Health Records

A patient's complete medical history in one place — read-only from their perspective. Everything written by their dermatologist is stored here and accessible at any time.

Two tabbed sections with live record counts: Medical Notes (consultation text written by the doctor after each visit) and Prescriptions (e-prescriptions issued through the platform)
Each prescription card shows the medication name, administration route (topical, oral), active status badge, associated treatment tag, and any additional instructions such as night only
Prescriptions linked to a completed or cancelled treatment are automatically removed from the active view
Connects to → Records are created by the doctor from the Medical Note and Treatment Plan panels on the Review Diagnosis page — they write directly here
Health Records

07

Treatment Plans

Patients track their ongoing and completed dermatology treatments in one place. Each plan is created and managed by the doctor and reflects the prescribed approach for the diagnosed condition.

Filter between Active, Completed, and All treatment plans
Each card shows treatment name, type (Topical, Oral, Procedural), current status badge, and start date
Progress log entries added by the doctor over time are visible per treatment, showing how the patient is responding at each stage
Linked prescriptions surface within the treatment detail, keeping medication and treatment context together
Connects to → Treatment plans are created by the doctor on the Review Diagnosis page and linked directly to the originating AI diagnosis record
Treatments

Dermatologist

Role

08

Doctor Dashboard

The clinical command centre. The doctor sees at a glance how many AI analyses are waiting for review, what's on today's schedule, and their overall validation track record.

Four stat cards: Pending Reviews, Today's Appointments, Total Validated, and Upcoming — all live from the database
Pending Reviews panel lists every unvalidated AI diagnosis with condition, date, confidence score, severity badge, and a direct Review button
Low-confidence cases (below 70%) show a Consultation recommended warning label, signalling the doctor to prioritise them
Today's Appointments panel shows each patient's name, visit type, time, and any emergency flag for the current day
Connects to → Clicking Review on any pending case opens the full Review Diagnosis page; Today's Appointments link into the Schedule view
Doctor Dashboard / Queue

09

Patient Queue

A dedicated full-page grid of all AI analyses awaiting doctor review. Designed for efficient triage — the doctor can scan condition, patient, confidence, and urgency before deciding which case to open first.

Grid layout with one card per pending analysis: condition name, severity badge, patient name, confidence score, submission date, and Pending Review status
Cases where AI confidence is below threshold display a Consultation required warning to guide triage priority
Filter between Pending, Validated, and All to track workload and review history
Review & Validate button on each card navigates directly to the full Review Diagnosis page for that case
Connects to → Each card in this queue was created when a patient submitted an image on the AI Analysis page
Patient Queue

10

Review Diagnosis

The most clinically significant page in the platform. The doctor reviews the patient's uploaded image alongside the AI prediction, then validates or overrides the result — and creates notes and a treatment plan — all from one screen.

Patient Information panel: full name, email, and patient ID for identity confirmation before any clinical action
Uploaded image displayed at full resolution with timestamp; AI Diagnosis panel shows primary condition, confidence score, severity badge, and a full bar breakdown of all four class probability scores
Confirm AI Diagnosis accepts the model output as the official record; Modify Diagnosis lets the doctor override the condition entirely with their clinical judgement
Medical Note panel: free-text consultation notes saved directly to the patient's Health Records the moment they are submitted
Treatment Plan panel: creates a named plan specifying type and instructions, linked to this diagnosis record and immediately visible in the patient's Treatments page
Connects to → Confirming or modifying flips the patient-side status from Awaiting doctor review to Doctor validated; Medical Note and Treatment Plan write to Records and Treatments respectively
Review Diagnosis

11

My Schedule

A chronological list of all upcoming appointments grouped by date. The doctor manages their clinical day from here — starting consultations and marking them done in real time.

Appointments grouped by date with a count badge per day — no calendar clutter, just a clean ordered list
Each card shows exact time, patient name, visit type, status badge, and urgency level (Urgent / Emergency) highlighted in red
Start and Done action buttons update the appointment state machine: Scheduled → In Progress → Completed
Emergency appointments booked by the patient appear with the Urgent badge automatically — no manual flagging required from the doctor
Connects to → Status changes here reflect immediately in the patient's Appointments page — the same record, seen from both sides simultaneously
Schedule

12

Patient Records & Clinical Actions

Doctors can access any patient's full medical history and perform all clinical write actions from within the platform — creating notes, issuing prescriptions, building treatment plans, and logging progress updates.

View a patient's complete EHR: previous consultation notes, all prescriptions issued, AI diagnoses, and treatment plan history
Create Medical Notes — free-text observations and follow-up instructions written after a consultation, immediately visible to the patient
Issue Prescriptions — e-prescriptions linked to a treatment plan, with medication name, route, instructions, and active status
Create and update Treatment Plans — specify condition, type, status (Active / Completed / Cancelled), and attach prescriptions
Log Treatment Progress — timestamped entries documenting the patient's response at each stage of the treatment timeline
When a treatment is marked Completed or Cancelled, its linked prescriptions are automatically removed from the patient's active view
Records / Clinical Actions

Admin

Role

13

Admin Dashboard

A platform-wide operations overview. The admin sees the health of the entire system at a glance — patients, appointments, AI analyses, and pending validations — and can act on urgent items immediately.

Four top-level stat cards: Total Patients, Total Appointments, AI Analyses submitted, and Pending Validations still needing doctor review
Alert banner surfaces the count of appointments pending confirmation, with a direct Review shortcut
Pending Appointments panel lists all unconfirmed bookings across the platform with patient name, date, time, type, visit reason, and current status
Quick Actions sidebar gives one-click access to Manage Patients, Manage Doctors, and All Users without navigating away from the dashboard
Connects to → Pending Validations count reflects the same queue the doctor sees in their Patient Queue — the admin monitors it but the doctor resolves it
Admin Dashboard

14

User Management

Full visibility and control over every account on the platform. The admin can search, filter by role, and activate or deactivate any user.

Searchable table of all registered users showing avatar initials, full name, email, role badge (Patient / Dermatologist / Admin), join date, and account status
Filter buttons for All, Patients, Doctors, and Admins narrow the table instantly without a page reload
Deactivate / Activate toggle on each row — deactivated accounts cannot log in until re-enabled by the admin
Row count shown above the table keeps the admin aware of the total user base size at all times
Connects to → Deactivating a doctor account immediately prevents them from accepting new validation assignments or logging into the platform
User Management

Platform-wide

Architecture

15

Role-Based Access Control

Every route, API endpoint, and UI element is gated by role. Patients, doctors, and admins each operate in a completely isolated context — enforced simultaneously on the frontend router and on every Flask API request.

JWT access token carries the user's role claim; Flask's @role_required decorator rejects requests that don't match on every protected endpoint
React Router's protected route wrapper reads the role from AuthContext and redirects unauthorised navigation attempts before the page even renders
Each role sees a completely different navbar, dashboard, and set of available actions — no shared UI that's just hidden with CSS
Role-Based Access

16

AI Model Integration

The TensorFlow/Keras model trained in the Jupyter notebook is loaded once at Flask startup as a singleton and reused across all inference requests — no per-request load overhead. A deterministic mock fallback activates automatically when the model file is absent, enabling full development and demo use without the model binary.

Model trained on four classes — Acne, Eczema, Milia, Rosacea — using MobileNetV2 transfer learning with a custom classification head
Preprocessing pipeline mirrors training exactly: resize → unsharp mask → CLAHE → normalise, using OpenCV on the server
Confidence threshold at 70% triggers a Consultation recommended flag, visible to both patient and doctor
All four class probability scores stored in the database and surfaced in the doctor's Review Diagnosis page as a bar chart
Model path → backend/app/services/ai_service/model/skin_model.keras — excluded from version control via .gitignore due to file size
AI Integration
Skinnovate React 18  ·  Flask 3  ·  TensorFlow / Keras  ·  PostgreSQL 16  ·  JWT  ·  Docker
